World.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
World.desc: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

World.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
World.desc: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

World.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
World.desc: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

World.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
World.desc: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

World.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
World.desc: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

World.title: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
World.desc: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

Technology.title: White House and Anthropic hold 'productive' meeting amid fears over Mythos model
Technology.desc: The discussion is a sign the AI firm's technology may be too critical for even the US government to do without.

Technology.title: Tinder and Zoom offer 'proof of humanity' eye-scans to combat AI
Technology.desc: The tech aims to identify people's irises and stop the rise of fake accounts and malicious scams.

Technology.title: What is Claude Mythos and what risks does it pose?
Technology.desc: The company's claim the AI tool can outperform humans at some hacking and cyber-security tasks has sparked fears in the financial world.

Technology.title: Could a digital twin make you into a 'superworker'?
Technology.desc: Firms say digital twins make staff more productive, but are they a potential legal minefield?

Technology.title: Things can't go on like this with online safety, Starmer tells tech bosses
Technology.desc: It comes as the government continues to consult on whether to ban under-16s from social media in the UK.

Technology.title: Booking.com customers warned of 'reservation hijacking' after hack
Technology.desc: The travel platform said it had changed Pins to protect customers but would not say how many were affected.

Science.title: Fears for 1,000 toads after reservoir drained by water company
Science.desc: Volunteers who helped more than 1,500 toads cross a busy road to a breeding ground fear they have died.

Science.title: Artemis II crew: 'We left as friends - we came back as best friends'
Science.desc: The four crew members gave their first press conference since they splashed down nearly a week ago, and emphasised hope and unity.

Science.title: Why cheap power could matter more than clean power in the push for net zero
Science.desc: The question of how important making our electricity clean is to going green is coming under increasing scrutiny

Science.title: Butterfly numbers are dropping but here are five species you may see more of
Science.desc: A warming climate has helped some to flourish, researchers say, but the outlook is troubling.

Science.title: New footage shows moment Orion capsule hatch is opened at sea
Science.desc: Newly released video shows the moment the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule is unlocked to a joyful reunion with the four astronauts.

Science.title: Golden eagles' return to English skies gets government backing
Science.desc: The birds could be reintroduced as early as next year following a £1m injection from the government.
